texto: The white feminist becomes the CEO. The black feminist becomes the exiled rebel. The white feminist speaks about teaching literacy like i should thank her, hold her hand, kiss her for teaching children of darker skin. The black feminist should be grateful. The black feminist wears her natural hair, she is called ‘too rebellious’. The white feminist cuts her hair, she is brave. The white feminist gets featured on TIME. The black feminist is the fine print.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ijeoma Umebinyuo: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The white feminist becomes the CEO. The black feminist becomes the exiled rebel. The white femini…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que gratidao, feminism, racism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e racism.

texto: Stop the idea that a woman’s beauty is for a man’s gaze, that you have the right to touch her. This idea that she must smile and accept unwanted approaches even when she is clearly uncomfortable. Just because you call a woman beautiful does not mean you have the right to behave like her beauty belongs to you. There are women healing from scars gotten from men who have called them beautiful yet offered them pain. The beauty of a woman is hers and hers alone. There are triggers for some women, respect this and know this. The beauty of a woman is hers and hers alone.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ijeoma Umebinyuo: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Stop the idea that a woman’s beauty is for a man’s gaze, that you have the right to touch her. Th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, feminism, respect pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de respect e womanism.

frase_original: Stop the idea that a woman’s beauty is for a man’s gaze, that you have the right to touch her. This idea that she must smile and accept unwanted approaches even when she is clearly uncomfortable. Just because you call a woman beautiful does not mean you have the right to behave like her beauty belongs to you. There are women healing from scars gotten from men who have called them beautiful yet offered them pain. The beauty of a woman is hers and hers alone. There are triggers for some women, respect this and know this. The beauty of a woman is hers and hers alone.
